The tape almost comes off the roll by itself and does not have much sticking power. What's going on? We have been using this for years and have always been extremely happy with the product and we use a lot of it.. We also bought a roll at a local big box store in... Review 1: rating 5.0. Such a great item for leg/knee-surgery patients! After knee-replacement, my husband needed to ice his leg a lot. He also needed to 'wrap' the leg to protect the staples and incision. In the hospital, they used ace bandages over the skin, then the 'ice pad' from the ice-water circulating machine, then ANOTHER ace bandage to keep that in place. With all those elastic bandages, the wrinkles they were causing his skin where they bunched up got really painfully annoying, and putting them ... Review 2: rating 5.0. Super Tubies! Tubie-grips are amazing. I first encountered them when I had to go to wound care for a non-healing ulcer. They used tubies over the gauze-wrap bandage on my leg. It served two purposes: 1) the gauze bandage stayed where it had been put, which if you've ever tried to wrap something in gauze, you know it slips, and 2) it served to gently compress the entire area of the leg that it covered, preventing it from ballooning up and actually reducing the existing swelling.

To the 1-star reviewer that mentioned not having any threads when using this with a drill press: you can't tap with a drill press. The tap needs to be able to pull the drill down as it threads the hole. Otherwise, you'll just get a hole with no threads. So my first step was to make a little fitting for my lathe that would allow me to chuck up the M6 bolt. That’s where these taps came into play. It was pretty easy work to drill a pilot hole in a piece of brass rod, then use the M6 tap to enlarge the hole and cut threads. The design evacuates... Review 10: rating 2.0. Cheap Not so great. reference_output:
rating 5.0. Loads of Fun Experimenting When I read that the others had batteries that had to be charged after an hour's use, I knew the Seek would be better for me because it uses your phone's battery. I hiked for three hours and while it drained my iPhone 11's battery to about 30% by the end (I took a lot of photos), I was prepared with a portable charger just in case. It turns out I didn't need it.

I'm a herper (amateur herpetologist) and took the Seek into the desert to see if I could locate any snakes or lizards. They sometimes hide in crevices in between boulders in my area. I knew they did this because it holds heat. But until I took the Seek into the desert, I didn't realize just how much.

Posted are only two of many filters. You'll see I tested one of the filters on my desktop computer that was clearly heating up the door as it vented. One photo is of the living room window. You can clearly see which window had sun exposure that time of day. I was really quite surprised at how well the plant life holds the heat during the night. It was 46 degrees when I took the saguaro photo (not the crested saguaro). All of the shrubs around it were warm, as well.

Using a different filter, you can see where the crested saguaro held heat. The boulder photos were amazing to see. I knew they held heat. I could feel the temperature difference when I touched it, but to see it like this was very cool.

I can't wait to go back out and explore more!

I plugged the Seek into my phone and it immediately prompted me to download the software, so I did. I also watched the video Seek provided within the app. It was very helpful. I registered the product. It does ask for access to your photos, which is fine because I want the photos saved to my phone, which it does.

You can see from the photos that it's a very small device. It comes with a waterproof case and a key ring so you can attach the case to wherever you like. Ignore the hair tie that I tied to the key ring, I need get a carabiner for it. On a side note, it clicks at regular intervals as it's testing the temperature and setting the image, I presume. It didn't bother me at all, it's quiet.

I plan to experiment with the other filters. Well worth the money, in my opinion. It clearly has household uses, but for me, exploring nature with Seek is what's going to be fun. If you have kids, take them out with this and let them see nature in a way we don't normally get to see it. Science....let's find out!

Review 1: rating 5.0. FAST DELIVERY... I am stunned at how quickly I received this product! I have yet to use the actually product because the items I bought to apply it to have yet to arrive. I bought all products in the same 30 mins period, and the shipment was just so speedy on this it will be two more days before I get the products to apply it to! Thanks for the speedy service! Review 2: rating 4.0. Blinding white light! Ok, hopefully I will never know if this product helps me avoid being hit by a bloody cager, but I can say that I went home and tested it in the dark. It's really cool that it is as black as black can be in regular light, then in the dark you shine some light on it (or flash camera you can see really good then) and it is an almost blinding white. Cool stuff. Review 3: rating 5.0. Fantastic product; a lifesaver This is an outstanding product! She didn't want a backpack with already sewn-on reflectors... Review 4: rating 5.0. Great stuff! I moved to Boston and now I can walk everywhere instead of driving. That convinced me to give it a try. There's quite a lot of tape included in the package. I only used one strip for my bag, and I think the rest could easily be enough for 4 or 5 more items.

Sewing it was relativel... Review 5: rating 5.0. bright the reflective cloth works great! comes in different widths so you have the ability to customize and not waste too much product. Anyway I did expect something a little more revolutionary, like solid deep opaque black that magically became blindingly reflective with any light source in a dark environment. Not exactly the case here. BUT, what I will say is that it's a good price point for the product and ... Review 7: rating 5.0. Exactly as expected This stuff is just like it's described. In normal daylight it looks black, but shine a light on it at night (or take a picture using the flash) and it lights right up. I sewed it onto my black bike jacket and nobody could even tell it was there during the day, which is what I wanted. But it really helped with visibility at night. Review 8: rating 5.0. Wow!!! This is honestly the brightest reflective cloth that I have ever seen. It reflects just as advertised. I will update after getting this product sewn onto my jackets and putting it through some use. Can't tell much durring the day but helps meet the military requirement to ride on base at night. Review 10: rating 5.0. Perfect for home made bicycle raincoat. This stuff is perfect if you live in the Great Pacific Northwest, are a bicycle commuter and don't want to look like one of those twerpicle yuppy bicyclists in flaming yellow.

What I did was buy a [[ASIN:B000QC8R2A Red Ledge Thunderlight Rain Jacket]] in black and some [[ASIN:B00063X38M 3M 08008 Black Super Weatherstrip Adhesive Tube - 5 oz.]] then I just cut and glued this tape to the jacket.

Works great. The only reason ... Review 11: rating 5.0. This stuff is amazing I had this sewn onto my club vest (black leather) and it blends in amazing during the day. Visibility is the reason we're not usually seen and get hit -- this is one great way to help with it. The picture of it "glowing" in light from another source really doesn't do it justice.... this stuff is BRIGHT when a light hits it. Review 12: rating 5.0. i used these in the army I hate those damn orange and those yellow vests the units try to force you to buy from the p.x. so I being good at adapting and over coming so bought this and a black leather vest from leatherup.com cut the strips to what I wanted ( one down the middle of the back and one across and one diagonal in each direction the put a big army seal right in the middle over the tapes) and took them to alterations with all the patches I wanted on it only got asked once if ... persona: This customer prioritizes high visibility and safety in low-light conditions, values durable and versatile reflective materials that can be securely attached through multiple methods, and is willing to invest time in proper installation for long-term performance.
reference_output: rating 5.0. If you want to be seen at night, buy this! This reflective is SO bright. I was a bit skeptical when I pulled it out of the box, but then I put it in front of a headlight. WOW! It is not actually tape; it needs to be sewn on. It is strips of reflective material. I've hand sewn it, used a sewing machine on it and used material glue. All of the gear that I have used this on has held up just fine. It is not a deep black, it is subtly lighter than that, but that is because of the reflective properties. I've sewn it onto riding jackets and bags, both black. While you can see the strip, it basically looks like it's supposed to be there. And, it is a very non-invasive way (the complete opposite of a high viz vest, but just as noticeable) to add a huge margin of safety when riding at night. When I run out, I will definitely buy more (there is a TON of this stuff in the package!). I couldn't be happier with this stuff.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Just the fan sound remains, amazing! Upgrade was not hard. Took about an hour, half of it removing my existing printer mods, OctoPi, camera, etc., labeling some of the cables with a marker and taking plenty of photos along the way, just in case. I'd say much easier than the initial printer assembly.

I cannot believe how much smoother and quieter the steppers are on the new board. I was going to say whisper quiet, but I can hear a whisper. The only sound now is the fan.

Initial board ... Review 3: rating 5.0. Great seller Excellent customer service. When I installled it on Ender 3 pro, I was hesitant that the LCD screen said Ender 3 only and did not show the pro, but the seller Cathy assured me that it’s the same software 1.1.8 for both printers. Very satisfied with the seller. Review 4: rating 5.0. This is the QUIET version Just installed the board and started a 20 hour print. Unbelievable the difference. Basically all I can hear is the fans. I used to always know when a print stopped because I could hear it two rooms away. Now, you have to visually look at it to know when it is done. Worked perfect from the second I installed. Review 5: rating 5.0. Silent Printing It's amazing how quiet this board makes my printer. I had expected some noise reduction, but nothing like this. I usually sit with my back to the printer and now I feel I need to turn around every once in a while to assure myself that it's still printing. All I can hear is the fans.

It took about an hour to replace the old board with the new one. It would have probably gone quicker, but the old board's connections were hot glued in place and I had to break... Review 6: rating 5.0. Easy to install, works like a charm! It took me about an hour to remove the old board and swap out to this new one. It worked right away without any issues. I would highly recommend this board if you want to upgrade your Ender. Review 7: rating 5.0. Super quiet! When I first started printing after upgrading to this board, I had to check to make sure things were working properly because it was so quiet. Works great! Review 8: rating 5.0. Must Have! The constant high pitch sound that comes from the stock stepper motors started to drive me insane after a few months. This board is an easy drop into place (about 45-60min install due to the insane amount of glue that comes on the stock board) and the TMC 2208 stepper drivers are silent. The fans are now the loudest thing on the machine and totally tolerable. Must have! Review 9: rating 5.0. Makes you printer completely silent!!! OMG!!!! I cannot believe how quiet my ender 3 is now!! You can only hear the part fan!!! Review 10: rating 2.0. No bootloader installed, in spite of Creality's claims. Arduino required to flash bootloader. Despite claims by Creality, mine arrived with no bootloader installed. Arduino required to flash bootloader.

Mine had no bootloader installed, and secondarily went into a boot-loop after 5-10 minutes of being powered up. I have since purchased another from a different vendor. It also had no bootloader installed but otherwise worked fine. Regarding the boot loop, I think I just got... reference_output:
rating 3.0. Great stuff Works just as advertised.. I repaired a souvenir mug someone got me.. The handle broke into 3 pieces.. I got it all together.. Held for 1 min..

Because I was a bit messy, the handle is 'rough to the touch' I don't want to use any solvent in order to remove. I am afraid I could weaken the bond..

I didn't have an issue with clean up. But I will wear plastic gloves next time while using this. And I will watch the videos available online demonstrating this product.

UPDATE: 2 months later. The excess adhesive turned rust colored. I gently scrubbed off excess. I was left with some reddish cracks.

My mug held together for a few months.
The adhesive did NOT hold
Up to microwave use.

My take always are.
This product is not useful to repair a mug for kitchen use. I don’t know if the problem was dishwasher and microwave.
My feeling is it doesn’t like the microwave.

I will only use this product on ornamental items in the future.
Then again, superglue and other cheaper adhesives will also work just as well maybe?

persona: This customer prioritizes sturdy, user-friendly products that excel in weight distribution and stability on level ground, and they value practical performance for everyday carrying tasks, though they are mindful of limitations when navigating stairs or lifting heavy items vertically.
reference_output: rating 4.0. Works great on a level surface, not so good for use in carrying large items up stairs These are sturdy, easy to figure out and use. They do a great job of weight distribution when an item is being carried on level ground. When you go up stairs these can present a challenge. For instance, my wife and I were carrying a large chest (all drawers removed) up steep stairs where the elevation of the top of the chest was at 4 feet above its top for the person on the bottom of the stairs. We ran out of strap length for the part that goes underneath the object being carried so we had to remove the straps when going up stairs and simply grasp the item by its base. So, these are useful when carrying on a level surface, they can sometimes be of little use when carrying a long object up a steep set of stairs as you run out of strap length for the strap that runs along the length of the item being carried.

Review 4: rating 5.0. I have a window a/c that wasn't cooling like it used to I have a window a/c that wasn't cooling like it used to. I took the cover off , sprayed the coils with this and rinsed with water. Now it blows out 48 degree air and cools as good as ever.

Review 10: rating 5.0. Simple to use appears to work well. Easy to use cleaned the coils on my RV’s air conditioners. I need to try it on my home air conditioner. It works fast I used a pump spray it only took a few minutes. A lot of dirt came off the coils I repeated it a second time. Did not get as much dirt off the coils on second try but it does look much better.

Review 11: rating 5.0. Bang for the buck and works well It cleaned up the condenser coils (outside unit) very well. Used a dilution of 1:2 (coil safe : water). Tons of sand and dirt came out. I unscrewed the top fan grate so I could get at the coils from both sides. After a couple applications and letting this stuff sit for a little while, I rinsed the coils off a couple times with the flat setting on a garden hose (do not use a pressure cleaner! The high pressure could destroy the formation of the coils)....

Review 12: rating 1.0. Seller Listing Of Product Use Description Is Misleading! For AC Evaporator Not AC Condenser! I'm NOT happy. Listing for this says it cleans ac evaporators AND condensers. No it doesn't as I found out after using it per the directions of 1 to 5 ratio. Before I started I cleaned off what I could with my hand and a soft condenser brush. I used it the first time and was like that's it? So I waited two hours and did the whole process again and between the two cleanings it hardly did anyth...

Review 13: rating 4.0. Woked pretty good This product worked pretty good on evaporator coils and okay on condenser coils. It took 4-5 applications on both units to get good results. Nu-brite works better on outdoor condenser coils, but is less safe to use than this product.

Review 14: rating 5.0. Easy to use and great results. I usually clean my AC outdoor unit at least once a year. I usually use some rather toxic stuff that isn't safe for anyone let alone pets. Since last time, i gained a fury child and needed to be careful on what I used. For the amount of product this makes and cost along with how well this works, it is a great buy. I used a pressure sprayer that I use for weed control and that worked fine. I let it sit for a good 10 minutes and washed the dirt off with a ...

Review 15: rating 5.0. Best product you will find for cleaning air conditioner coils! I diluted per instructions into hand sprayer and applied product to heavy mold coated air conditioner coils. The mold washed completely away with a light sprayer of water from my water hose. By gar exceeded my expectations. Ordering more to have on hand.

Review 16: rating 5.0. This stuff REALLY works! I have only window units in my home and the fins were nasty. I sent away for this. It came with a stiff bristle brush and a metal 'comb'. You just mix the concentrated liquid as directed, brush off any loose dirt from the fins and then spray it on the fins, let it sit for a couple of minutes and then 'comb' the fins with the metal tool. No rinsing! My window unit cools so much better and I believe that this is the reason my power bill went down by almost $50!

reference_output:
rating 5.0. I love It It's HUGE! I'm thinking of getting another one for the kitchen. I got this one, because of sheer volume, for my craft room. But, I really like it. We cannot have any open trash cans, because of the pooch. Flip top, or swing top work. This one is a great price. I spent $80 on the metal one for the kitchen, but I'm thinking of shifting that downstairs and using one of these, for sheer size, and the ease of being able to just hose it out if I need to. It looks nicer than you'd expect. I got the bronze one, and it doesn't even look plastic if you're not right up on it... I definitely recommend this item for price, looks and functionality.

I did actually end up buying another one for the kitchen. That great big bonus going to be great! We’ll use the other for recycling.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Works well, shrinks nicely. Good product, right amount of adhesive and has a good number of sleeves even in the larger sizes. Great value. It shrinks without too much glue spilling out. I’ve used it 2/0 wire and smaller stuff and they worked well. Review 2: rating 5.0. Awesome!!! Perfect for what I needed. Shrink up nice and adhesive lined for a confident waterproof seal. These are the best and affordable too! Review 3: rating 5.0. Quality heat shrink at a killer price! Compared to some brand name counterparts, this heat shrink works pretty well. The wall stiffness is good, the shrinkage is good and the glue adhesion is very obvious when you hear it up enough. Used the 3/8 diameter tube to seal the battery cable lugs to the cables for my boat batteries so far (other projects in the future for mt truck). Will be buying more later. Review 4: rating 2.0. Seller was quick to ship - Product lacks quality control! The seller was great - provided quick service. The product, however, lacked quality control. All the black pieces were slightly smaller than their red counterparts. I've used shrinkwrap pieces before to install sound systems, and know that 3/8 pieces fit over the banana plugs. These red pieces fit perfectly, as expected. The black pieces, however, were too small to fit over the banana plugs. Then I looked through the ent... Review 5: rating 5.0. Nice heatshrink with good adhesion I really like the glue in these because the application I am using the heat-shrink for is more targeted at reinforcing the crimped connections as opposed to waterproofing, though I'm sure they would be great for waterproofing as well. I built a power distribution panel and used the heat-shrink on the weather pack style connectors to help ensure the connections remain secure for a lifetime. reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Worked great on my water hose repair that had a peanut size hole. 4 layers worked just fine. Review 2: rating 5.0. Fair price, delivered faster than expected. This stuff ... Fair price, delivered faster than expected. This stuff really works. Fixed a water leak in my RV until I could replace the tubing Review 3: rating 2.0. I wanted this to work, but unfortunately it did not. I bought this for a temporary landscaping drip line fix. I followed the instructions exactly, but it did not work. The water pushed through the stretched and overlapped silicone tape. Disappointed that it was money wasted. Review 4: rating 5.0. Incredible stuff, make sure to stretch it tight though. User for 5 years. If you've never used it, you're a dedicated user now, just amazing, and, make sure that you stretch it tightly on the wrap, it'll fuse and seal. Review 5: rating 5.0. USE IT THE RIGHT WAY AND YOU'LL BE AMAZED! I loved this silicone tape since the first production of it which I believe was called F1 tape or something military. Had a stripe on it. With this brand I was skeptical but now I see it exceeds my expectation and works exactly the same if not better. Now to the part why people rate this product with low stars; using it wrong!

How to properly use it; okay so if your trying to fix something leaking water...first start off with dryi... Review 6: rating 5.0. Best repair tape! I've tried many of the cheaper imitations. None of them stick like Rescue Tape. Wrap it stretch it and it sticks. Other tapes I've had to try over and over and it's touch and go. It works about 1/3 of the time. Review 7: rating 5.0. Worked for me! worked great. All I had was a simple leak from a drain pipe that leaked after a lot of use from thanksgiving. Make sure you have clearance around the entire pipe...you are supposed to pull it as tight as you can around the pipe. go slow and make sure to not twist and overlap half on pipe half on previous tape while you continually stretch. Review 8: rating 1.0. Ok It's rated for 900psi I doubt, I used on steering high pressure fluid hose,, I cleaned the hose & stretched the rescue tape as much as I can, and overlapped by half on previous turn, but it could not hold the fluid pressure, fluid still leaked. Review 9: rating 5.0. Handy to have around Great tape. I use it all over the place. Amazing what you can find a use for it. Just make sure you stretch it tight if you are trying to make a repair on something like a water hose. Review 10: rating 1.0. Wouldn't seal air leak Tried to repair 1/4 inch air hose with 50 psi pressure. Rescue Tape does not stick to anything other than itself, so the air leak travels under the tape and comes out at the end of the tape repair. The stretchy Rescue Tape also stretched and bulged at the location of the leak. Ordinary electrical tape that sticks to the air hose worked perfectly. Because Rescue tape does not stick to whatever is being taped, it's hard to imagine how it could ever seal a leak... Review 11: rating 4.0. Repair was successful At first it was hard to get control of the tape and the plastic film on the one side. I needed three arms. Once I figured out how to handle it the tape made a great repair and saved a 100ft extension cord I have had for 30 yrs. and is trained to coil easily. I would buy it again and get my wife to help, maybe. Review 12: rating 1.0. Does not seal sprinkler drip systems. It did not seal the leak in the sprinkler system at all. Probably not useful for any water under pressure Review 13: rating 5.0. This stuff is AWESOME! You can use it for anything This stuff is AWESOME! You can use it for anything. Just follow the directions. I wrapped some plier handles to cushion the grip. I put some on my 5 gallon bucket handles. I used some to cushion my rifle rack hooks on my ATV. I used some around a low pressure plumbing leak on my shop deep sink. The list goes on and on for uses!! Review 14: rating 1.0. Melts at 300 The product can't go up to 500 degrees as is melts at 300. Review 15: rating 3.0. Generally works as advertised, but I have now had ... Generally works as advertised, but I have now had two separate instances of the end of the tape coming off itself, and beginning to peel for a few inches. I thought that it was supposed to bond permanently to itself. The first few layers hold. If I can find a way to get the entire length of tape to bond, I will come back and revise this review. Review 16: rating 4.0. Backup Protection I've seen the product demonstrated and it looks good. I purchased it for unexpected situations that arise. Review 17: rating 5.0. Five Stars Good stuff to have on hand. Review 18: rating 1.0. One Star Thus tape sucked its just ruber thats not even stickey. Review 19: rating 5.0. Oh man, this tape. This the the tape that forged Narsil into Andúril. This is the tape that holds the very foundations of the continents together and healed my broken dreams. This tape is so strong I called it daddy by accident.
When the nukes fall and the cockroaches are evolving a new society in the radioactive wasteland, this is the lost technology they'll build their Utopian paradise on. Review 20: rating 5.0. Rescue Tape I once split a radiator hose about a 3 inch split, no lie, wrapped it with Rescue Tape, filled radiator & drove it 50 miles home. It was dry as a bone & I ran it for a few weeks until I got around to putting a new hose on. Many uses.
persona: This customer prioritizes long-term durability and permanent fixes, favoring high-performance repair solutions that deliver reliable, leak-free results even under repeated use.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Wow! This thing is amazing. So basically, the pipe under the bathroom sink just broke clean off. It’s as if someone took the metal pipe and just cut it straight through. The only way to fix would be to replace or weld. Along with the JB Weld putty, I used this. First the putty and once that was dry, this just in case. I will never have to make this repair again! It’s been a month now and got and cold water has been ran numerous times and not a drop!

For practicing interrupted suture, running suture, matress (Donati) and other types of "epidermal" surface are excellent! The nylon sutures run a bit more "stuck" than in real skin. Silk runs well.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Easy to assemble and reconfigure. Exactly what I was looking for in a hand truck.

When I examined it I could see that these wheels were never going to stand up to an...

Review 6: rating 5.0. They work great. Best hand truck for the money Lightweight and can take TONS of abuse. We use these at my work delivering 50 lb boxes. They work great. Best hand truck for the money.

Review 8: rating 5.0. Light weight and can use indoors. Quality product. Very happy with purchase . Made moving much easier. Nice and light weight. My wife can use it also.

Review 13: rating 5.0. Serves its purpose and worth the money I used this to unload a full PODS container, and I wouldn't have been able to unload the container without it.  It was easy to assemble and capable of moving large, heavy boxes.  You can either use it upright or you can change the handle's position so it can be used as a 4 wheel cart.  The handle is also adjustable when in the upright position.  I will definitely use this the next time I move. It was worth every penny.

persona: This customer values functional utility and practicality in products, particularly for household repairs, pest control, and dietary management, but is willing to tolerate complexity and messiness if the product delivers on core functionality, and they prioritize real-world performance over ease of use or aesthetic neatness.
reference_output: rating 3.0. Just OK, hard to put together and not that stable So this does the job, but it is hard to put together.  The pins you need to bend to secure the axles are quite thick and hard to bend.  I ended up having to use a combination of a chisel and needle nose pliers to get between the pins and bend them, but I'm not sure if I bend it enough to keep the wheels on.  Once assembled, it's awkward to use, the plate is a little too narrow, the handle hard to adjust, and the whole thing a little wobbly, but it did get the job I needed it to get done, albeit with more force on my part than I thought I could muster and than I'd hoped would be necessary.  They just don't make these like they used to.  I used to have one from last century but it disappeared in my last move.  My guess is one of my former movers is using it, as it was nice and solid.  I miss it, this is a poor substitute.

Strong enough to resist shape deformation even with lots of oil After a plastic pan flexes on you and spills oil everywhere, you will wish you bought this pan. The only downside is that since it is metal, it does get hot and will transfer that heat to the hands. So either wait for it to cool down if you drain hot oil into it, or use gloves to pick it up. Next, I drill a one-half inch hole in the side and fix in a evaporative cooler float. The list cooling is adequate for all but heaviest cutting but much less mess to deal with than flood cooling. I really like this solution. Review 3: rating 4.0. Great stuff. It does exactly the same job, and keeps my tools just as long as the stuff 5 times the cost.

rating 5.0. Works great as a flood coolant, long sump life I use this coolant as flood coolant on my lathe. I have ZERO RUST ISSUES ! The sump life is 2 years plus. No smells and excellent tramp oil rejection...

I have a bucket of expensive Boe Lube coolant, but I will never use anything else in my lathe. COOL BLUE IS THE WAY TO GO.

Anyone that has rust issues doesn't know how to use the product. Don't blame the product for operator error...

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. A good upgrade for the Ender 3 I recently switched to direct drive, putting a bit more weight on the x axis with the stepper and added parts. I decided to look into what options were available for dual z for added rigidity to take on the extra weight. This product was one of the only ones with Prime shipping but with lack of any reviews I was skeptic.

The overall quality is good. All parts were included but it does lack directions, though in one of the above pictures they ... Review 2: rating 5.0. A great upgrade that is easy to install. I wish the ender 3/pro had come from the factory like this. I'd noticed that my x gantry didn't always stay level. Even after messing with it several times it just didn't always move perfectly. This fixed all of that.

The hardest part is making sure your gantry is level before tightening everything down.

One final note, you may want to print a power supply relocation bracket before installing since you will need to move y... Review 3: rating 1.0. Belt is too small, makes kit unable to be used. [[VIDEOID:a9619f41259147f43974abfddc225b7c]] Belt is FAR TOO TIGHT and makes the kit pretty much useless. Even in the install video, they skip over the part where they struggle to install the second pulley using editing. Great idea, poor execution. It's so tight that there's no way that the stepper motor can overcome the load added. Review 4: rating 1.0. Belt is too small, makes kit unable to be used. [[VIDEOID:a9619f41259147f43974abfddc225b7c]] Belt is FAR TOO TIGHT and makes the kit pretty much useless. Even in the install video, they skip over the part where they struggle to install the second pulley using editing. Great idea, poor execution. It's so tight that there's no way that the stepper motor can overcome the load added. Review 5: rating 5.0. Super service from the supplier The belt supplied is around 4 teeth short.

Definitely not made for the ender 3.

Edit: The supplier has returned with a solution and for that I will give 5 stars Review 6: rating 2.0. Belt too short Like a few of the other reviewers mentioned, the belt I was supplied is just too tight for the printer to function. The tensioner is not even installed. The belt installed per the video is as tight as a drum. I'll order a belt that slightly longer and put the tensioner to use. Seems like a good kit other than the belt. Review 7: rating 5.0. Updated Review: Had a issue, but resolved very well. New Review >> Dec-2020, I wrote an "ok" review for this product, the seller reached out to me and we discussed the issue. Overall, very simple - the initial belt supplied was a bit too short. Because it was short, when assembling it, I had to pull everything inboard slightly to get it to fit and that caused some binding. Long story short, the seller was in constant contact and has provided a slightly longer (2mm) belt. After ... Review 8: rating 1.0. Belt too short. Binds the Z Axis when it gets near the top. Cant print tall objects As others have said, the belt is too short. The vendor says they are shipping a longer one. Well their not. I just got it today and installed it (9/10/2020). The Z Axis shafts are closer together at the top then they are at the bottom. It would work for shorter prints both nothing tall. I sent the vendor a message about it to see if they would send me a longer belt to fix the issue. If I get ... Review 9: rating 2.0. Belt is too tight It’s a good idea but the belt is too tight… Needs one or two extra teeth or a couple extra millimeters Review 10: rating 5.0. Fantastic upgrade for your 3D printer I purchased this kit to use with a highly modified Creality Ender3. My X gantry is now 400mm wide, and dual Z was highly desirable for keeping things nice and level. Some folks use dual motor kits, but that requires recalibration of the motors on a regular basis. Mechanical dual-Z seemed like a much better option, and this kit did not disappoint. I should add that I also have 420mm of Z travel now, so I HAD to do something one-off.

So, it all works as advertised, assembly is a pain, but your bolting it into an ender 3, so you should have expected that coming.. If you got your machine together right the first time, levelling all the bits correctly, then you will get this done as well. If ya get all haphazard with it, I could see how it would bind, but that has to do with your printer as well.

The belt is fine, the 2nd z axis is left f... Review 12: rating 4.0. Power supply grounding The product requires you to relocate the power supply not a problem for me however please note the power supply needs to be grounded to the printer chassis. I did it by taking a wire off the power supply case and onto the screen mounting bolt it's taky but it works. Review 13: rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY THIS This item is a complete scam and low quality product sold on Amazon. The bearings were way too small that you could not fit the z axis rod into the bearing, literally could not even force it. Wrong parts! Belt was obviously
Too small, the bearing for the center guide was missing an insert sleeve. Worst product ever, I am 100% certain any of these positive reviews are fake or paid for. Review 14: rating 4.0. Solid product worth buying I noticed the added stability and accuracy immediately. I have been printing cable chains and when the print head would zig zag quickly over a narrow space, the X axis would shake a tiny amount - I could see and hear it. The prints still came out great, but I knew I could get better and ditch the annoying shaking sound. This product was the solution. Your printer and prints will really benefit from this upgrade.

Two things keep this product from ... Review 15: rating 4.0. A must have upgrade if you add direct drive. This is a must have if you changed your ender 3 to direct drive. The print quality is much better. I will add one warning that is not the fault of the people that make this kit. The top brackets don't necessarily align with the servo position. They align with the center of the vertical supports. The problem is that the creality printers aren't built to exact specs so the left hand lift screw does not set exactly in the center of the v... Review 16: rating 3.0. Made a big improvement...but hard to recommend. First, let me say that this Z axis mod made the single biggest improvement in printed objects that I've done so far. Layer to layer is very much improved due to stability of the extruder. But there were two problems that prevented the kit from even working at all as delivered.
The first item has been mentioned before and is entirely unforgivable. Without a collar under the bearing on the new drive screw....it will allow the pull... Review 17: rating 5.0. A must, even more so if you go direct drive extruder. I would say this is a must for an Ender 3. From what I can read online, they all have sag on the right side of the gantry. Installing this kit will completely eliminate that sag, in turn making your bed and prints that much more leveled out. After install, don't forget to level the gantry one last time. Am easy way to do this, on the right side worm gear, loosen the set screws for the belt, and at the same time raise the gantry. T... Review 18: rating 1.0. Missing parts. I did not receive all of the parts in my kit. The bearings were missing. Both z lead bearings and the 2 belt tension bearings were not in my kit. Other than that the kit seems great. Review 19: rating 5.0. My printer is not only level, it's square. Not only is my bed actually level with this modification, it's actually square!

All Cartesian 3d printers (Ender 3, CR-10, etc.) that only have one side of the x-gantry being pushed up are going to suffer from some sag on the side without the lead screw. This means that no matter what, when you level your bed, even if you level it perfectly, it won't actually be square along the axis. All your prints will have be slightly leaning ... Review 20: rating 4.0. Definintely helps with print quality but no install instructions! Being a noob to the 3D print world, you keep reading about what mods/upgrades are suggested for better quality prints as well as what will make calibrating your machine or printing easier. It was suggested that for the Ender 3 V2 to install a dual z axis kit and after watching a "billion" youtube videos over and over I decided on the kit with the belt instead of a second motor. My decision was really based off of 2 t...
persona: This customer prioritizes functional reliability and practical performance, often upgrading or modifying products to meet specific mechanical needs; they value cost-effective solutions that deliver consistent results, are willing to make DIY adjustments when necessary, and favor items with proven durability and compatibility across diverse applications.
reference_output:
rating 3.0. Seriously under-engineered, the driven lead screw is allowed to float up and down. I wanted a belt drive and not a second stepper motor as the latter can get out of sync.
This kit doesn't do anything to hold the right side lead screw fixed allowing it to float up or down, thereby making it a useless mod. The left side is held captive by the stepper motor bearings and can't move.
I needed to add a collar to the new lead screw below the upper bearing and along with two plastic collars that were in the kit, I clamped it with the the pulley, a bit rube-goldberg but it works.
The belt is just long enough to make it between the two screw centers so no tensioner was necessary.
I also found it necessary to swap out the 8mm nuts with brass anti-backlash parts to get rid of all the play. A 8mm handwheel makes it easy to manually position the Z axis.
My machine is an Ender 3V2.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. Need instruction on how to assemble it. These small fishing magnet does not come with any instruction which seller definately need to include one.
I save everyone the trouble and google proper way to assemble these. The main screw goes through the bottom of the magnet, on top put on the flat washer, then the lock washer follow by the loop bolt. You will need a Philip screw driver to tighten it.
The magnet itself works fine. Not sure it holds 75# but works for my need. Review 2: rating 5.0. Not falling off the fridge! My wife likes to put pictures and calendars and notepads for shopping lists on the fridge and they fall off all the time. These will definitely do the trick and keep anything you stick on the fridge secure. Review 3: rating 5.0. Small and Strong Don't let the size fool you. These are six strong magnets. I ordered them to help locate nails in the soil. Using the provided eye bolt, I tied each magnet to string and dragged it through dirt I reclaimed from a dry pond and used to level depressions in my yard. Apparently there were rusting nails and other pieces of iron in the soil which presented a challenge to my mower's tires. By systematically dragging these magnets over the soil I was able to remove any rusti... Review 4: rating 5.0. Very Small but Very Strong The material in these magnets is new to me and seems to be as strong as a magnet of a much larger size. I am very pleased with this purchase and will do business with this seller again. Review 5: rating 5.0. Incredibly useful in my camper van I recently bought a "blank slate" mini cargo van with the cargo area being almost completely bare metal - floor, sides, and ceiling. I wanted to outfit it as a camping vehicle without spending a small fortune to have it professionally converted. (Nor do I have a shop or the skills to do a fancy conversion myself.) I watched a DIY camper van video where the owner made extensive use of rare earth magnets. Wow, what a brilliant idea! I initially ordere... Review 6: rating 5.0. Super strong and well built. Worked perfectly for what I needed them for. Review 7: rating 5.0. Strong magnets The magnets were plenty strong for my use. I used them to hang up a projection cloth screen over steel storage cabinets. They held strong with no worries. Review 8: rating 3.0. . Tested on dumbbells. These are 30lb magnets sold as 75lbs Review 9: rating 5.0. Want to check to see if you accidentally threw a steel can into the aluminum bin? Here you go. Tied some parachute cord onto one of these & found that the Weruva cat food cans are steel after going “fishing” in my aluminum bin. Another one is glommed onto my Kitchenaid mixer where it lets me check items for steel content. They are versatile & will positively secure almost anything to your refrigerator. Buy them now - you can’t go wrong unless you drop one on your old cassette tap... Review 10: rating 2.0. Returns are not possible. Product defective. One of the loops had no threads so was useless with screw. The company cannot replace parts and magnets cannot be returned. I needed all six so The set was useless. I ordered another so we’ll see. Review 11: rating 1.0. No where near 85# more like15-20 These were clearly NOT 85# magnates Review 12: rating 5.0. Wow are these strong! I used 2 of them to hold my ham radio under the seat. I didn't want to have to make holes or take the seat apart to attach or remove it and these were the perfect solution. Holds super well. As a matter of fact, I have to really pull to get it off. I think they're definitely worth the price. Review 13: rating 5.0. Ideal Having read other reviews i wasn't sure if these would be strong enough. There may have been a manufacturing defect with those reviews, but i personally didn't have any issues with mine holding securely to a roll cage bar. I am using four of them mounted through a sheet of thin plexiglass where two magnets were a little questionable and i was able to remove the utv windshield fairly easily, but with four it's very secure now. So a single magnet might not be quite strong enough ... Review 14: rating 5.0. Strong Magnets Used to mount spice rack on the side of the Refrigerator and that rack is not going anywhere.
May of been a little over kill on the magnets, was worry about the rack falling. Review 15: rating 1.0. 25 lbs maybe, definitely not 85 lbs Two important things to note on these magnets:
1. They are NOT returnable!
2. I'm not sure what the "ideal conditions" are, but I was only able to lift roughly 25 pounds with one of these. There's no way you will get anywhere close to 85 pounds of lift. Review 16: rating 5.0. Nothing Short of Amazing I have metal ceilings in my apartment so these things work perfectly for hanging mt monkey light and disco ball. They are crazy strong and extremely durable. Very happy with these.
persona: This customer prioritizes high performance and durability in their purchases, values well-packaged products, and is willing to pay for quality, but is frustrated by poor product consistency and lack of return options for damaged items.
reference_output: rating 1.0. 2/ 6 magnets broken, can't return because they're magnets 2 of the magnets are broken and you cannot return them because they are a hazard to ship.  Guess I'm stuck with these now, thanks!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Hobbyist recommended. I think this stuff works for most applications, good for repairs, but I use it to assemble knife handles, so it works well for hobbyists as well. Review 2: rating 1.0. DOES NOT WORK THIS DID NOT WORK AT ALL Review 3: rating 5.0. This stuff lasts I fixed a handle of a Dishwasher and glued the front mirror of my Jeep Wrangler. In both cases - after 6 months - no issues.
I am living in southern Louisiana the heat, Humidity is taking on me a bigger toll than on this stuff. And I have the Chance to get inside in the AC - mt Jeep not!
It lasted trough the summer. I tried other products, and they lasted in the winter less than a week.
It is reusable and I still have 80% left. Review 4: rating 5.0. it sticks! This epoxy is amazing! I used it to bond glass to rubber and it adhered with a strong and tight seal. It took about 5-10 minutes to set as advertised, and it was about 24 hours until it fully cured (although I was comfortable moving my project inside after about an hour without worrying about the adhesive failing). The smell is about as strong as any epoxy, just be sure to use it outside or a well-ventilated area when working, and use gloves if you can. It definitely dries... Review 5: rating 5.0. Holds Extremely Well Under Wide Temperature Swings I used this to hold the suction mount for my navigation to the hard plastic part of my dashboard. Normally, I don't like epoxy mix glues because (1) they are messy, (2) they don't dry properly if not mixed exactly right and (3) they take a long time to set & dry. However, after trying and failing with super glue and silicone glue, I tried this after reading the many reviews. GREAT STUFF! First of all, it comes out mixed in the co... Review 6: rating 1.0. Didnt work The product did not work and this is the second J-B weld product that did not work. Great marketing on how it works on everything but in reality it does not. Review 7: rating 3.0. Clear vs Original When compared to the original it is not quite as good. Lower adhesion property. Review 8: rating 5.0. Worked well on ceramic dish Used it to fix a broken ceramic bowl. I have never used epoxy before this, but it was easy to use and appears to have done a good job. Review 9: rating 2.0. Laundry Basket Fail... I am a big JBWeld fan and have used their other products for years. This stuff was a major disappointment. I bought it specifically to fix the handle on my wife's laundry baskets. I tried a simple glue joint and it couldn't even support the weight of an empty basket. Fine, whatever. I assumed the reason it didn't stick was due to the smooth surface of the plastic so I hit it with some 150 grit sand paper and cleaned it up with alcohol then applied a very libera... Review 10: rating 2.0. Not what I expected I don’t like the way you have to use & mix this glue it’s messy. And This glue smells super bad. I glued a frame it’s fixed but it’s not my favorite glue. Would not buy it again Review 11: rating 5.0. Good stuff!! I thought for SURE this wasn't going to come out of the "chambers" easily, and would prob be clogged. But nope - came out together and easy as pie! Even comes w/ an applicator (looks like a mini popsicle stick - perfect!) to apply the stuff. I just used a small paper bowl to mix it, applied it to some chipped shower tile, and voila! Done! Review 12: rating 5.0. Good choice in glue...so far. I needed a glue to piece together a broken china bowl. The two parts of the glue come out simultaneously, making mixing the appropriate quantities of each rather painless. I was able to apply the glue with a toothpick and hold the pieces together long enough for initial setting to take place. Only time will tell how well the pieces stay together. Two days later, so far, so good. Review 13: rating 1.0. Did not work for an aluminum. Repair Did not work. Let it cure fore 24 hours and fell apart. Original JB Weld works great and so does the JB kwik for my needs. Review 14: rating 1.0. Impressively ineffective WOW. This was about as effective as silly puddy. No, it does not cure in five minutes. It starts to harden in five minutes. In one hour, it is still gummy, and does not do the job it claims. Maybe it takes overnight. I don't know. But this stuff is garbage. Review 15: rating 5.0. Worked for Kintsugi I’ve used this for a modern ‘Kintsugi’ style porcelain repair, with satisfactory results.
I wanted to repair a chipped old bowl to reach a Kintsugi style repair while using modern accessible materials. I decided to try the combination of epoxy and golden colored mica powder.
I mixed some powder with the epoxy and applied it in small quantity on the chipped part. I had to factor in the epoxy viscosity, gravity and the relatively quick setting time to get ... Review 16: rating 2.0. Failed on metal to glass I used this glue for nightlights. I glued my fused glass 3X3 panel to the fastener. Out in the sun, during a craft show the glass came unglued from the metal piece. you can see the imprint of the glue on the glass. Review 17: rating 5.0. Seems to work great Seems to work great! Cures nice and clear.
There is a bubble in each side - so I suggest either storing it always upright so you never get a bubble out the tip so you get equal glue, *or* store it upside down and burp it and get all the bubbles out so in the future you get equal amount of glue. Review 18: rating 5.0. Fixed my Iron Arch with Ease One of the finials on my iron archway rusted through. I tried fixing it with E6000, which lasted one season. I didn't want to replace the entire arbor so read up on better adhesives for the job. This epoxy was just the fix. Easy to express equal amounts of both, comes with a wooden spatula for mixing, and sets quickly. I taped the finial in place and left it overnight. Once the tape came off, the finial was solid. Would recommend! Review 19: rating 2.0. Downsized plunger system makes it harder to measure equal amounts ClearWeld always worked well for me and I assume it's still a good formula, but J-B Weld has downsized it so small (for the same price, of course) that a Hershey bar -- the old downsized cliche -- looks massive in comparison. The problem is that the plunger-driven openings are so small, it's hard to see whether you're squeezing out an equal amount of epoxy and hardener. That was always something of a problem because yo... Review 20: rating 4.0. Very nice product It did dry crystal clear and was really strong, I attached metal hardware to heavy acrylic plexi blocks. Very nice product.
persona: This customer prioritizes performance, reliability, and precision in 3D printing and related accessories, consistently seeking high-quality materials and components that deliver consistent results, support advanced customization, and integrate seamlessly with their existing setup—willing to invest in premium products that enhance functionality, durability, and print success rates.

rating 5.0. Works great, a very strong bond I used it to fix a broken leg on a laptop lap desk I have. It was broken at the leg hinge. It holds up really well, I can even fold the leg now. I didn't expect it to work that well.
I'll add a picture soon.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Best borescope/endoscope I have used with my phone. This is my third endoscope. By far this is the best. It has the right amount of stiffness for most jobs. My other units would typically need me to tape stiff wire along the cable to get stiffness.

It is a bit futsy connecting to the wifi, and if the unit turns off you have to reconnect to the wifi. But there are advantages that I think make it worth it. For one thing, both my son and I could connect at the same time.... Review 2: rating 1.0. One Star cheap junk.. had parts rattling around inside the case right out of the box Review 3: rating 5.0. It saved me hundreds of dollars! I had a water leak in my wall. I didn't know where it was and the Endscope enabled me to find it by drilling 1/2 inch holes in the wall. I was amazed when I found the water pipe with the hole in it (see image). Hiring a plumber could have cost me over $500 , but I was able to isolate the problem and fix the leak for less than $100 (including the cost of the scope). Review 4: rating 5.0. Outstanding value and best gift of the season! I purchased this for my 65 year old father who is an aircraft mechanic. He has several bore scopes costing several hundreds of dollars and is never without his ipad, hence this purchase. Instead of having to carry around those large bore scopes, I thought he could carry this with him when looking at engines and behind instrument panels. This is perfect. My dad had a little confusion with the password to connect to the camera WiFi (just... Review 5: rating 4.0. It is easy to set up and use Works exactly as described. The only draw back is not being able to use wall charger to charge or have an indication of the battery charge level. Works perfectly with my Samsung phone. It is easy to set up and use. The semi-firm wire is very useful. Review 6: rating 5.0. Not a bad camera. Happy with it. Pretty happy with it. I wish the view was a little more detailed. You'll only get 640 x 480 but on the other hand, you're not spending a lot of money here. I'm also using an iPad so the picture would probably be better on a smart phone due to the smaller screen? This camera sees best when you're about 2 inches from your target. Happy with the cord. It is semi rigid along the entire length so it can be bent and shaped the way you need it to be and it'... Review 7: rating 1.0. Buyer beware This thing is junk. When I got it, I charged it for an hour or so and then tried using it. 15m later the camera light went out and the video froze. I connected the device to power via a USB power outlet. The device turned on and I could connect to its SSID but the camera light would not turn on and there was no video. I then disconnected my phone from the SSID, turned the device off, closed the app, turned on the device, and reconnected to the SSID. 5m later the video fr... Review 8: rating 4.0. Nice device Works perfectly as described (Used with Google Pixel). The only problem I've got is the camera gets very hot within seconds of turning the camera on. Review 9: rating 4.0. Unbeatable price but false advertising I struggled with how many stars to give this. Bottom line is it does a great job for the price. But it is not 2 megapixel. Max resolution is 1280x720, which is 1 megapixel. But still, it's been working very well for me and video quality has been very good for what's needed. Review 10: rating 3.0. Medium Quality for a great price. Great unit for the price. The resolution is not good enough for detailed work, but the unit works well for the intended use. Review 11: rating 5.0. Five Stars Great product, much better picture than the contractor type. I use it to check drain pipes and inside walls. Review 12: rating 5.0. I like it! First weekend I got it I used ... I like it! First weekend I got it I used it to check out a blown up kx125. The mirror attachment for the camera kind of sucks though, the straight view is much better. Easy to connect to. Review 13: rating 5.0. Password problems This seems to be a very good uni, resolution is excellent and seems to be soild !! Be sure to use only the password that is printed on the sticker that is fastened to the device and don't try to "modify" it as seems to be suggested when you open the app, if you do ....you're screwed and the camera will never work again, at least that was my experience. The seller was very good in making it good with me. GREAT seller !! Thank you !! Review 14: rating 4.0. Ok, but not spectacular Directions that came with camera specified it should be charged with a specific power block, yet it wasn't included. Says it can be charged with a computer which is what we ended up doing. Didn't seem to hold charge all that long after we fully charged. An hour tops maybe?? Probably not the best quality camera, but acceptable for the price. Review 15: rating 5.0. Better than I expected Easy to set up and works perfectly. Review 16: rating 5.0. Great product and Company. I have upgraded My[[ASIN:B01MYTHWK4 Wireless Endoscope, Depstech WiFi Borescope Inspection Camera 2.0 Megapixels HD Snake Camera for Android and IOS Smartphone, iPhone, Samsung, Tablet - Black(11.5FT)]] review to 5 stars, Depstech contacted me and sent me a new scope free of charge, and the new one has been working great. Depstech is an A+ company and I highly recommend them. These scopes are handy in many situations and I would recommend them for all hous... Review 17: rating 4.0. Product review updated - great product, great company to work with After leaving my 1 star review the company contacted me directly via-email. They gave me the option of a full refund or a replacement product. I chose to replace the product as I really wanted a working Endoscope. I am pleased to say the replacement device arrived within two days and works flawlessly. The endoscope does everything I was expecting it to do and works as advertised. I am also pleasantly surprised to... Review 18: rating 5.0. Great scope, great price I've only had the scope for a week, but so far it seems to be working great! Resolution is clear and it's very helpful when I'm inspecting my gun barrels. Review 19: rating 5.0. Such a smokin deal!!! Follow the link nstructions, charge up the endoscope portion via the mini USB, pair up the wifi and you're there. It really couldn't be easier.

Great resolution and small size. 10 years ago this was big and bulky with its own screen and $1000.

Look at the price and buy it! Review 20: rating 4.0. Finding more and more uses for the product. I bought this product for a specific use in the home (to locate a dead mouse under our kitchen cabinets) but I have subsequently found a growing number of uses for it in the shop. Very happy with the purchase.
persona: This customer is motivated by value and practicality, often making purchases during sales, and prioritizes a product’s functionality, portability, and long-term utility, with a strong preference for items that deliver consistent performance and become essential over time.
reference_output: rating 4.0. Worth it and I would buy it again. I like this unit. I’ve been meaning to get one for awhile now I saw this one on sale and decided to get it. Picture is clear small enough to get most anywhere and will hold its shape. That’s a good and a bad thing at times but no reflection on this product just different things are needed for different jobs. I have used it more than I imagined I would and now I don’t ever intend on being without one.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Hmmmmm Well, I read reviews saying that you should have something grow as some say every house has mold.  I used this and didnt have anything so I am assuming that we have no mold.  However, not really sure since it kinda seemed like it didnt do anything...?  Not sure if it worked or not.

Review 2: rating 5.0. Good product This was good way for me to find out if mold in my house. You have to send off and pay to get results but it was worth it to know I didn't have harmful mold.So I would purchase this again if needed.

Review 3: rating 5.0. Five Stars this really works but costs to much to maintain.

Review 4: rating 1.0. Not useful I purchases to check for mold in an HVAC system. I tried several different test. It does not tell you you will have to pay for additional testing and send  them away.  Very disappointed.

Review 5: rating 1.0. More cost than expected Dos realize there was a forty dollar charge to have the test analyzed

Review 6: rating 5.0. Doesn't get better than this Doesn't get better than this. Be aware that you do have to pay for lab test results. Duh! Of COURSE you have to pay for lab tests. This is the kit that puts it together and explains how to do it. There isn't a little botanist in the box to give you free lab results. We signed up on-line for email results, paid for expedited shipping to Florida, and expedited test results. The whole process took maybe 3 days. By submitting the nasty results to our landlord...

Review 7: rating 1.0. Fake I didn't even expose mine to anything and it grew mold so that's means it already had mold on it

Review 8: rating 1.0. Scam- Used Test Kit I believe the kits I received were used, cleaned, and sold as new.  The petri dish was dirty on receipt.  And, the growth medium never gelled - it just stayed liquid.  Guessing they replaced the genuine stuff with sugar water or something similar.  I can't send this for testing if it doesn't gel.  Nor could I have used it for the HVAC vent test if it doesn't gel.  Should have chosen the more expensive version - this one is only cheap because they are scamming peop...

Review 9: rating 1.0. I have tried numerous times to get in touch with ... I have tried numerous times to get in touch with Pro Lab and no one emails or calls back. I still owe for the second kit. Right now I have paid for the $50 test and 2 kits 9.99 each and have nothing. Never returned my calls or email!

Review 10: rating 1.0. Must buy 2 You have to have 2 of them. At the time I ordered description did not state. So I returned

Review 11: rating 2.0. Didn't notice the extra fee for identifying the mold, took longer than 48 hours to show 5 kinds of mold. Ehhh..not real impressed. I did catch some mold spores in our bedroom, but we really didn't notice anything in 48 hours, except a couple of tiny, gray dots. We knew we had mold. I let it sit about a week (too long for the test), and there's 5 kinds of scary-looking mold. But if you want to know what mold you might have, you have to mail it in with $40. reference_output: rating 3.0. I guess I feel like it kinda sucks... Ack! I was so disappointed with this! I use it for shlepping all of my stuff at craft fairs, but this really sucked for that. For starters, the wheels are pitiful. They really only work well on a flat, hard surface. They're not good for steps or grass (or even sidewalk cracks) WHATSOEVER. Second, the metal part that folds out for the dolly setting is too small/shallow. Not very helpful. The cart itself is just not good for anything remotely heavy duty or for any uneven terrain. It also kept getting stuck in the folded position. Yes, I read the instructions, and I'm a competent person. The thing was stuck. But it does fold up to be very compact, so there's that! I saw so many rave reviews, so I guess I'm confused!

reference_output: rating 2.0. High Quality / High Price : Rubbermaid was a high flying Stock in the early 2000's but their High Prices gave an opening to many competitors who have taken Market Share from them. Rather than finding ways to reduce manufacturing costs and lower Prices, Rubbermaid continues to overcharge on products that are readily avaialable from Competitors and 35% less. I bought this Trash Can because the size was what I needed at the time, even though it was too expensive. Since ordering, I have found a nearly identical Product for $21 versus $32 for the Rubbermaid Trash Can. I plan to return it given this large Price Difference. A trash can is a trash can ------ not much difference between competing products. Would Not Recommend . . . .

reference_output:
rating 1.0. Does not set. Doesn't harden. Read the one-star reviews. Do not buy. I've used two-part epoxy for decades, never had a problem. This one never fully 'sets" (hardens, cures, dries). Yes, I used equal parts hardener and resin, still soft. I glued a broken bowl, clamped with ribber bands -- even after 2 weeks it was still so spft I could scrape off the excess with my fingernail.
Look up all the one-star reviews, you'll see many similar complaints.
I do not recommend this epoxy (no matter how inexpensive it is).

This is a pretty simple device Measure white noise machine in baby's room.

This is a pretty simple device; it'll measure the current noise level. If you're trying to measure some short duration sound (fireworks maybe?), you won't find any sort of trend measurement. However, if the sound you want to check is nearly constant, this will work really well for you.

For exhausted parents: After reading some articles about how dangerous some white noise machines could... Review 16: rating 5.0. JUST GET IT! It seems to work very well! Review 20: rating 5.0. Not bad It is good for the price, easy to use. It would be hard if not impossible to do an 8-hr TWA with it but it is great for spot checking which is what I needed it for
persona: This customer values high-quality, functional tools with practical features like durability, clear readability, and versatile mounting options, and is willing to pay for reliable performance and long-term usability.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Great tool. This meter gives you a usable unit of measure to all forms of sound from ambient noise all the way up through your neighbor’s car with no exhaust.  The meter has a mount point for a tripod or camera attach point, which allows you to position the meter and still read it. It has an auto shut off feature too.  The display is large and easy to read.  This was worth the money.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Great filament Prints fine on a mk3s, great shiny color instead of being a terrible off-yellow. Review 2: rating 5.0. Looks Great If You're Patient Very hard material to work with, it seems to prefer sticking to the nozzle more than the print bed. I've had the best luck with adherence on my CR-10 when the bed is perfectly level and heated to 60C and the nozzle is heated to 220C.

Sort of a yellow color when viewed head on, works best on objects with multiple faces and angles. Definitely the closest PLA I've found to... Review 4: rating 5.0. Stunning! reference_output: rating 2.0. Hit or Miss I've purchased 2 rolls of this. Both the color and shine of the filament were amazing and I love the look. - .0135” with backer, .009” after backer is removed. That’s .23mm. (This is twice the thickness of blue painter’s tape.)

reference_output:
rating 4.0. It's OK. But designated bags DO NOT FIT I have 3 Bissell Zing vacuums. They are inexpensive and do the job well, although suction could be a little better. This most recent one is a newer model. It takes the same bags as the older models... But its bag holder does not easily accept these bags -- they are too tight to properly fit into the side rails in the new vacuum's bag holder. The only thing I can do is peel off one of the three plies of cardboard on the bag's side tabs -- and then carefully fit it into the side rails. Even then I sometimes have to TAPE the bag onto the side rails so it doesn't fall apart when being used. WHY? If I could, I would expand those side rails to accept the cardboard tabs of the bags-- but the side rails are plastic and I don't want to break them.
As for suction, I needed to vacuum small piles of fine dirt on my balcony -- I had to remove the wand and use the curved part of the upper hose to get adequate suction .
The floor attachment is just...O.K. Nothing more than than ho hum suction.
Since I'm on a budget I have to take what I can afford. Once I can afford to buy a better one, these Bissells will be history.

Looks like great equipment I bought this to add new grommets to my 10x20’ storage tent. The covering is the same as a tarp you’d buy at a hardware store.

Useless Review 9: rating 5.0. Best grommet tool ever. This thing is the bomb!!! It worked even beter than i had hoped.
